audit_result.proof_trace: The C function does not validate that input arrays (commitments_bytes, cell_indices, cells, proofs_bytes) actually have num_cells elements. Lines 838-840, 858, and 894-896 index/copy arrays assuming this length, causing out-of-bounds reads if a caller passes mismatched lengths. All examined language bindings (Go:548, Node.js:877-879, Python:637-650, Java:683-701, Rust:528-548) validate before calling, but a direct C/C++ caller or incorrect FFI usage could bypass validation.
audit_result.attack_scenario: A C/C++ Ethereum client or code using FFI calls verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch with num_cells larger than actual array lengths. The function reads out-of-bounds memory at lines 838-840 (cell_indices), 858 (commitments_bytes memcpy), and 894-896 (proofs_bytes), potentially using garbage data in cryptographic verification, causing incorrect acceptance/rejection of proofs, process crash, or memory disclosure.

PROP-6a4369e9-inv-037.text: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch must receive consistent-length arrays for valid verification.
